article_meta.title: Iranians block a street during a protest in Tehran, Iran on 9 January 2026

reasoning: The model mixes quoted/source-attributed language with author prose, commits multiple attribution violations, and uses non-standard bias labels. Some observations (source selection emphasis and vouching for a rights group) are defensible, but key author-level bias instances were missed.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Items 2 and 4 flag quoted or clearly attributed statements (e.g., cousin’s account of beating; rights group director’s "crimes against humanity") as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: One item is clearly a false positive (framing quoted accusation as author stance). Another mixes author phrase with quoted language. Others are arguable.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Misses defensible author-prose bias such as the unqualified motive claim "Doctors are being arrested... for helping save lives" and the unnamed "one human rights organisation" (potential omission of source attribution).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels used (Selection Bias, Loaded Language, Appeal to Authority, Framing Bias) are outside the defined set and mis-map to allowed types like Bias by Omission/Story Choice, Word Choice/Sensationalism, and Slant/Opinion as Fact.
